note: ANALYSIS entry synthesizing BlarAI's OpenVINO measurements on the Arc 140V against community llama.cpp/SYCL figures for the same silicon (pulled 2026-06-27, primary sources + 3-vote adversarial verification). REFRESHED 2026-06-28 with the (a) on-hardware re-benchmark: Qwen3-14B + Qwen3-8B + Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B re-measured (5+2 runs each) on the current stack WITH the new pp (prompt-processing throughput) metric, so prefill is now comparable too (with the prompt-length caveat below). The blarai_measured numbers below are the 2026-06-28 figures; the May-2026 figures live in PERFORMANCE_LOG.md history.

hardware.gpu: Intel Arc 140V (Xe2, 8 Xe2 cores), integrated, shares system RAM (no dedicated VRAM)

runtime_versions_2026_06_28.ovms_30b: OVMS 2026.2 (OV backend 2026.2.0-21902, GenAI backend 2026.2.0.0-3121) — a separately installed, NEWER OpenVINO than the venv
runtime_versions_2026_06_28.correction: The original 2026-06-27 caveat assumed the box had moved wholesale to OpenVINO 2026.2. It had not: the in-process assistant path is 2026.1.0; only OVMS (the coder 30B server) is 2026.2.

methodology_note.blarai_metrics: generation throughput = output_tokens / total_latency (tok/s); TTFT = time-to-first-token (ms, a LATENCY); pp = prompt-processing throughput (tok/s, ADDED 2026-06-28) = input_tokens / prefill_time via a max_new_tokens=1 probe. 5 measured + 2 warmup + 30s thermal cooldown (benchmark_gpu_inference.py + benchmark_ovms_http.py, prompt-set v1).
methodology_note.community_metrics: llama-bench reports pp512 = prompt-processing THROUGHPUT (tok/s) and tg128 = generation THROUGHPUT (tok/s). Mostly single-run blog/forum figures.
methodology_note.comparability: Generation (BlarAI gen tok/s <-> community tg) is directly comparable. Prefill is NOW comparable too (BlarAI pp <-> community pp512), RESOLVED 2026-06-28 — with one caveat: BlarAI's in-process pp is measured over ~970 chat-template-formatted tokens (incl. system prompt), the OVMS pp over ~421 bare-message tokens, so cross-model pp is directional, not a controlled A/B.
methodology_note.cross_runtime: BlarAI numbers are OpenVINO (GenAI 2026.1.0 in-process; OVMS 2026.2 for the 30B); community numbers are llama.cpp/SYCL/Vulkan/IPEX-LLM. Same Arc 140V silicon, DIFFERENT runtime + different models/quants — a triangulation, not an A/B.

blarai_measured.config: speculative decoding ON (+ Qwen3-0.6B-pruned-6L INT8 draft)
blarai_measured.prefill_pp_tok_s: use spec-off pp ~1960 — the spec-on probe (1308) is a ContinuousBatching single-token artifact, not the true prefill rate
blarai_measured.note: UP from the stale 2026-05-22 figure of 13.6 tok/s median — the tuned pruned-6L draft on the current stack. speculative_decoding_active=True confirmed.

blarai_measured.config: spec-off / spec-on (+ same 0.6B draft; shared Qwen3 vocab)
blarai_measured.note: First spec-on number on record for the 8B; the 0.6B draft accelerates it too.

blarai_measured.architecture: qwen3_moe (128 experts, 8 active/token, 48 layers; ~30B total / ~3B active)
blarai_measured.moe_profile: FASTEST decode (38 tok/s, ~2x dense 14B) + FASTEST TTFT (214 ms), but SLOWEST prefill (pp ~480, ~1/4 the dense ~1960) — decode touches ~3B active params, prefill routes every token across all 128 experts (~30B compute).

generation_tok_s.approx_range: 8-16 (image-size dependent; conflates vision prefill + text decode)

community_llamacpp_arc140v.quality: secondary, single-run, almost certainly CPU not iGPU

cross_comparison_generation_by_size.note: beats community 8B (12-17.5 tg) by ~1.6x; the 0.6B draft is the lever
community has NO clean 14B-on-GPU number; the ~4.3 figure is likely CPU. 17.1 vs the May 13.6 is a real stack/draft gain
fastest decode of all; beats every community dense-7B; MoE active-param advantage

prefill_pp_comparison.blarai_dense_14b_8b_pp: ~1960 tok/s (over ~970 formatted tokens, OV GenAI 2026.1, spec-off)
prefill_pp_comparison.blarai_moe_30b_pp: ~480 tok/s (over ~421 bare tokens, OVMS 2026.2) — MoE prefill pays full-expert compute
prefill_pp_comparison.finding: BlarAI dense prefill ~1960 is ~3.7x the community llama.cpp/SYCL 7B pp512 (~536) on the same silicon. The MoE 30B prefill (~480) lands near the community dense-7B pp512 despite 30B total params — the active-param win is a DECODE win, not a prefill win.
prefill_pp_comparison.caveat: Different prompt lengths (970 vs 421 vs 512) + different runtime versions — directional, not a controlled A/B.

findings: pp (prefill) is now MEASURED (2026-06-28), closing the gap the 2026-06-27 analysis flagged: BlarAI's dense prefill ~1960 tok/s is ~3.7x the community llama.cpp/SYCL 7B pp512 (~536) on the same Arc 140V. OpenVINO's prefill is the strong half of its profile on this iGPU.

The 30B-A3B MoE has the mirror-image profile of the dense models: FASTEST decode (38 tok/s) + FASTEST TTFT (214 ms) but SLOWEST prefill (pp ~480) — decode is ~3B active, prefill routes all 128 experts. This is the single most useful shape in the dataset for capacity planning.
BlarAI's 14B is competitive AND fills a real community gap: 14B spec-on 17.1 tok/s (up from May's 13.6) where the community has no trustworthy 14B-on-GPU number (the ~4.3 figure is likely CPU).
Speculative decoding is a real lever on BOTH assistant models: 14B ~1.54x (11.1->17.1), 8B ~1.38x (19.8->27.4), both with the same 0.6B-pruned-6L draft (shared Qwen3 vocab).
OpenVINO quietly beats the runtime hobbyists default to: IPEX-LLM (their fastest turnkey Arc path) is archived/security-flagged (2026-01-28); Vulkan is ~5.5 tok/s on this iGPU.
BlarAI's numbers are the MORE rigorous side: 5 runs + 2 warmup + 30s thermal cooldown, vs the community's mostly single-run blog/forum figures.
caveats: Cross-runtime + cross-model triangulation, not an A/B (OpenVINO vs llama.cpp; different model families and quants).
Community figures are mostly n=1, single-run blog/forum.

Prefill pp is comparable as of 2026-06-28 BUT with a prompt-length confound: BlarAI in-process pp is over ~970 chat-formatted tokens, OVMS pp over ~421 bare tokens, community pp512 over 512 tokens. Each is internally valid; cross-model pp is directional.
Runtime versions differ even within BlarAI: 14B/8B on OpenVINO GenAI 2026.1.0, 30B on OVMS 2026.2. (This supersedes the original caveat that wrongly assumed a wholesale move to 2026.2.)
Co-resident cost NOT measured — each model was benchmarked alone (the 30B and the 14B cannot co-reside in 31.3 GB).
spec-on pp is reported as a ContinuousBatching single-token artifact (1308/1748); the spec-off pp (~1960) is the true prefill rate.

followup_a.what: Re-ran Qwen3-14B + Qwen3-8B + Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B on the current stack, 5 measured + 2 warmup runs each, with the new pp (prompt-processing) tok/s metric.
prep_edits: DONE (2026-06-27): De-hardcoded config_stamp model_name via --model-name + _derive_model_name so the 8B/30B runs label correctly.

DONE (2026-06-27): Added pp tok/s to benchmark_gpu_inference.py — a fixed probe (pp-v1) through the production generate path with max_new_tokens=1; pp = formatted_input_tokens / prefill_time; per-run unique prefix defeats prefix-caching; benchmark-only; unit-tested (test_benchmark_helpers.py, 49 passed).

DONE (2026-06-28): Wrote scripts/benchmark_ovms_http.py — the OVMS-HTTP 30B bench (loopback OpenAI-compatible, same prompt-set + pp probe, byte-identity asserted, unique prefix vs OVMS prefix-caching). Unit-tested (test_benchmark_ovms_helpers.py, 18 passed).
followup_a.run_params: 5 measured + 2 warmup, --run-cooldown 30. Recorded: in-process OpenVINO GenAI 2026.1.0; OVMS 2026.2; driver 32.0.101.8826.
followup_a.on_completion: DONE: added the 2026-06-28 PERFORMANCE_LOG.md measurement entry + a BUILD_JOURNAL entry + folded the fresh numbers into this comparison file.
